title: P1-1: corrupt state.json must fail loud, never be clobbered to defaults
action: Fix review P1-1. Add readStateStrict(root) to skills/bee-hive/templates/lib/state.mjs: absent state.json returns defaultState() exactly like readState; a PRESENT but unparseable/non-object state.json THROWS with an ERROR/WHY/FIX message (name the file, say the CLI refuses to rebuild state from defaults, FIX: inspect/restore the file — git checkout .bee/state.json — then retry). Do NOT change readState itself — hooks and bee_status depend on its fail-open shape. Switch every mutation verb in templates/bee_state.mjs (set, gate, worker add|update|remove|clear, scribing-run) from readState to readStateStrict; on the thrown error print the message to stderr and exit non-zero with the file untouched. Add test rows to templates/tests/test_lib.mjs: (a) corrupt state.json + set --summary x -> non-zero exit, file byte-identical after; (b) absent state.json + set still creates defaults (existing behavior preserved); (c) readState (non-strict) still returns defaults for corrupt input. Sync BOTH templates to their .bee/bin siblings byte-identically (lib/state.mjs and bee_state.mjs).

truths: A present-but-corrupt state.json is never overwritten by any bee_state verb — non-zero exit, ERROR/WHY/FIX on stderr, file byte-identical
An absent state.json still initializes defaults (existing tests keep passing)
readState's fail-open behavior for hooks is unchanged
Templates and .bee/bin vendor copies are byte-identical
key_links: All five bee_state mutation verbs call readStateStrict, none call readState
prohibitions: Do not weaken any existing test_lib.mjs assertion

trace.outcome: Added readStateStrict(root) to lib/state.mjs (absent state.json -> defaultState(), same as readState; present-but-unparseable or non-object JSON -> throws an ERROR/WHY/FIX message naming the file and pointing at git checkout). Switched all four bee_state.mjs mutation verbs (set, gate, worker, scribing-run) from readState to readStateStrict; readState itself untouched (hooks/bee_status still fail-open). Added 5 test rows to test_lib.mjs (lib-level absent/corrupt/non-object/non-strict-unchanged, plus a CLI-level corrupt-set-refuses-and-file-untouched case). Re-verified template<->vendor byte-equality via cmp.
trace.verification_evidence:
{
  "tests_inspected": ["skills/bee-hive/templates/tests/test_lib.mjs (readState/bee_state.mjs CLI sections)"],
  "tests_added_or_changed": [
    "readStateStrict returns defaults when state.json is absent (same as readState)",
    "readStateStrict throws on a present-but-unparseable state.json, naming the file and a FIX",
    "readStateStrict throws when state.json parses but is not a JSON object",
    "readState (non-strict) still returns defaults for the same corrupt input — fail-open shape unchanged",
    "bee_state.mjs set refuses to mutate a present-but-corrupt state.json (review P1-1: never clobber to defaults)"
  ],
  "red_failure_evidence": "Reproduced the pre-fix defect against git HEAD's bee_state.mjs/lib/state.mjs in an isolated scratch repo (own .bee/onboarding.json + a corrupt '{ this is not json' state.json): `set --summary x --json` on the OLD code exited 0 and overwrote the corrupt file with a fresh default-skeleton state (phase idle, summary 'x', gates all false) — exactly the P1-1 clobber described in review-findings.md. Running the identical fixture through the NEW skills/bee-hive/templates/bee_state.mjs exits 1 with the readStateStrict error and leaves the corrupt file byte-identical afterward (verified by re-reading the fixture).",
  "verification_run": "node skills/bee-hive/templates/tests/test_lib.mjs -> 148 passed, 0 failed; cmp lib/state.mjs .bee/bin/lib/state.mjs (silent/identical); cmp bee_state.mjs .bee/bin/bee_state.mjs (silent/identical)",
  "deliberate_exceptions": []
}
